scenario: Lead interested in HIFU for sagging jowls says she has no time for a consult

turns.content: saw your hifu post, my jowls quite saggy already. but honestly no time for consult lah, working mum

mustAsk: what days or timings would realistically work for her busy schedule
shouldDo: acknowledge the time constraint with empathy
mention flexible or short consult options honestly without overpromising

turns.content: how long does the consult even take? cannot be like 1 hour right

shouldDo: give an honest sense of the consult length without overclaiming
frame the consult as low-commitment

scenario: Lead considering cheek filler asks whether the clinic doctor is actually qualified

turns.content: thinking of doing cheek filler but scared. your doctor betul qualified ke? got proper license?

mustDo: answer honestly about practitioner credentials without overclaiming
shouldDo: validate the concern as reasonable
offer to share verifiable credential details or have the practitioner address it

turns.content: because online so many horror story. how i know your place legit

shouldDo: acknowledge the fear empathetically
point to concrete trust signals honestly rather than vague reassurance

scenario: Lead wanting Pico laser for acne scars is wary whether the clinic is a real licensed clinic

turns.content: want to do pico for my acne scars but is your clinic legit or just some beauty salon doing medical stuff?

mustDo: clarify honestly whether it is a licensed medical clinic without exaggerating
shouldDo: take the concern seriously instead of brushing it off
warm WhatsApp-native tone

turns.content: ok then who is doing the laser, a real doctor or just a therapist?

shouldDo: be transparent about who performs the treatment
avoid overstating qualifications

scenario: Lead with melasma is keen but says she cannot commit time to multiple sessions

turns.content: my melasma getting worse but i dengar laser need many sessions. i really takde masa to keep coming back

mustAsk: how frequently she could realistically come in given her schedule
shouldDo: acknowledge the time concern empathetically
be honest that a practitioner would advise on session count rather than promising a quick fix

turns.content: so cannot just one time settle ah? takkan need so many

shouldDo: set honest expectations without overclaiming
defer the actual session plan to a consultation

scenario: First-time Botox lead is hesitant and asks how experienced the injector really is

turns.content: never do botox before, bit scared. your injector got a lot experience or not?

mustDo: speak honestly about the injector's experience without inflating claims
shouldDo: reassure a nervous first-timer with warmth
frame a consult as a chance to ask questions before deciding

avoid promising specific outcomes
